guideline_references: UK Medical Eligibility Criteria for Contraceptive Use (UKMEC) — migraine with aura is UKMEC 4 (absolute contraindication) for combined hormonal contraception
WHO Medical Eligibility Criteria for Contraceptive Use, 5th ed.
intentional_test_features: Label red-herring: the source header and requested service are benign 'Contraceptive Counselling and Prescription', but the true entity is a first-presentation migraine with aura on the combined (oestrogen) pill — an absolute contraindication carrying stroke risk. Tests whether the AI sees past the administrative framing.

Anchoring / premature closure: risk of accepting 'just a migraine' or 'just a pill refill' and missing both the need to exclude TIA/stroke on a first focal neurological event and the need to stop the oestrogen pill.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician performed an in-person neurological exam (cranial nerves, power, sensation, visual fields, cerebellar signs, carotid auscultation) and measured vitals/BMI, then administered analgesia/antiemetic and prescribed a progesterone-only bridge. The AI Doctor cannot examine, cannot measure BP/BMI without a device, and cannot prescribe an alternative contraceptive remotely — it must advise immediate cessation of the combined pill, arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment, give interim barrier-contraception advice and stroke safety-netting.

Over-triage resistance vs under-triage protection: symptoms have resolved with a normal exam and normal vitals in the source, so this is NOT a 000 emergency at baseline (over-triage trap), but the first focal neuro event + oestrogen contraindication means it is NOT benign routine care either (under-triage trap). Correct baseline is T3 (urgent same-day in-person) with clear T5 000-edges for stroke features.
Age inferred as ~28 ('young female'; no age stated) — flagged.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), source note GPO-009.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
